step.title: Read CONTEXT.md
step.goal: Absorb the project's shared vocabulary glossary before formulating any questions or interpreting the user's request. CONTEXT.md is the source of canonical terms; mismatches between user phrasing and the glossary become questions for ticket-grill.

substep.title: Detect Glossary Layout
substep.action: Check the repository root for one of three states: 1. CONTEXT-MAP.md exists -> multi-context project. Read the map; defer per-context CONTEXT.md reads until ticket-grill identifies which context the request belongs to. 2. CONTEXT.md exists at repo root -> single-context project. Read it fully. 3. Neither exists -> no glossary yet. Note this in session state. DO NOT lazy-create here; the lazy-create policy belongs to ticket-grill (the first resolved term during Q↔R triggers creation per the context-md-format skill).

check.action: Read CONTEXT-MAP.md completely. Capture the bounded contexts and their paths. Store in session as {{session.contexts}}.

check.if: CONTEXT.md exists at root and no CONTEXT-MAP.md
check.action: Read CONTEXT.md completely. Capture canonical terms, aliases-to-avoid, relationships, and flagged ambiguities. Store as {{session.glossary}}.
Record {{session.glossary}} = "none". This is a normal state for early-stage projects.

substep.title: Display Glossary Status

section.title: Read-only here
section.description: Step 2 is strictly a read. Writes to CONTEXT.md happen during ticket-grill when a term is resolved through Q↔R. Lazy-create is the context-md-format skill's responsibility, not this step's.

section.title: Multi-context handling
section.description: For multi-context repos, ticket-orient does not pre-select a context. That decision emerges from the user request and the first round of ticket-grill. The map is loaded so the right context's CONTEXT.md can be read later without re-orienting.

section.title: Why this matters for grilling
section.description: A user who says "account" when the glossary defines "Customer" and "User" as distinct terms is making a question for ticket-grill to sharpen. Loading the glossary now means ticket-grill can spot these mismatches in real time instead of researching the same vocabulary twice.
